title: Maple Cured Canadian Salmon Roast
description: A fresh, flaky salmon roast with a sweet maple cure that pays homage to Canadian flavors while staying Whole30-friendly.
instructions: In a small bowl whisk together the sea salt, black pepper, minced garlic, chopped dill, parsley, lemon zest, and maple syrup until well blended.
Pat the salmon dry with paper towels and lay it skin‑side down on a parchment‑lined baking sheet.
Spread the maple cure mixture evenly over the flesh, pressing gently to adhere.
Wrap the coated salmon loosely in plastic wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes to allow the cure to penetrate.
Remove the salmon from the refrigerator, unwrap, and pat it dry again.
Drizzle the olive oil over the cured surface and place the sheet in the preheated oven.
Roast for 12–15 minutes, or until the flesh flakes easily with a fork and the internal temperature reaches 145 °F (63 °C).
Transfer the salmon to a cutting board and let it rest for 5 minutes before slicing.
Serve garnished with fresh thyme sprigs and a wedge of lemon if desired.
